etymology.description: semantic 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") + phonetic 其(其) (qí, "his") -> basket made of bamboo
etymology.interpretation: The character 箕(jī, "basket") combines 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") as the semantic component, indicating that the item is made of bamboo, with 其(其) (qí, "his") serving as the phonetic component to provide the sound. Originally, 其(其) (qí, "his") depicted a basket or container shape, and accordingly, 箕 specializes in the meaning related to a bamboo basket.

examples.chinese: 他用箕(他用箕)收集落下的叶子(收集落下的葉子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng jī shōují luòxià de yèzi.
examples.english: He uses a basket to collect fallen leaves.

examples.chinese: 箕的大小非常适合装米(箕的大小非常適合裝米)。
examples.pinyin: Jī de dàxiǎo fēicháng shìhé zhuāng mǐ.
examples.english: The size of the basket is perfect for holding rice.

examples.chinese: 那个老人总在集市上卖他编的箕(那個老人總在集市上賣他編的箕)。
examples.pinyin: Nàge lǎorén zǒng zài jíshì shàng mài tā biān de jī.
examples.english: That old man always sells the baskets he weaves at the market.
